name: North Carolina
tooltip: North Carolina — Cat 4: No codified statutory right of publicity. Common-law misappropriation may apply.
rightOfPublicity: Common law only. North Carolina has not enacted a codified general right-of-publicity statute. Common-law misappropriation may apply — elements vary, but generally: defendant appropriated plaintiff's identity for commercial value, plaintiff is identifiable, defendant received a benefit. Consult an attorney for state-specific application.
postMortem: No codified post-mortem term. Descendibility uncertain at common law.
remedy: Common-law misappropriation suit. Federal claims available regardless: Lanham Act § 43(a) false endorsement, defamation, and federal NCII statutes apply nationwide.
whatsInteresting: North Carolina hasn't built statutory likeness protection.
primarySources: Common-law cases vary by state; consult an attorney licensed in North Carolina.
Federal statutes and any state AI-specific statutes listed above.
